item.description: El mediocampista remató de cabeza en el área tras una contra letal. La selección campeona del mundo dio la vuelta al partido que ganaba 2-0 en los Octavos de Final de la Copa del Mundo.

media:content.media:title: Enzo Fernández anota el 3-2 para dar la vuelta a Egipto
media:content.media:description: El mediocampista remató de cabeza en el área tras una contra letal. La selección campeona del mundo dio la vuelta al partido que ganaba 2-0 en los Octavos de Final de la Copa del Mundo.

item.title: Enzo Fernández anota el 3-2 para dar la vuelta a Egipto

item.description: Comandada por Luis Díaz, y dirigida por Néstor Lorenzo, la Selección Colombia ya está en el Estadio de Vancouver para disputar el encuentro por Octavos de Final contra Suiza, en un juego que enfrentarán con máxima tensión.

media:content.media:title: Colombia ya sueña en Vancouver: enfrentan a Suiza en Octavos de Final
media:content.media:description: Comandada por Luis Díaz, y dirigida por Néstor Lorenzo, la Selección Colombia ya está en el Estadio de Vancouver para disputar el encuentro por Octavos de Final contra Suiza, en un juego que enfrentarán con máxima tensión.

item.title: Colombia ya sueña en Vancouver: enfrentan a Suiza en Octavos de Final

item.description: El mediocampista argentino se llena de emoción al anotar el gol del triunfo ante Egipto, se muestra agradecido y con la convicción de seguir adelante en la Copa Mundial.

media:content.media:title: Enzo Fernández: “Venía anhelando el gol desde hace tres años, soy un privilegiado”
media:content.media:description: El mediocampista argentino se llena de emoción al anotar el gol del triunfo ante Egipto, se muestra agradecido y con la convicción de seguir adelante en la Copa Mundial.

item.title: Enzo Fernández: “Venía anhelando el gol desde hace tres años, soy un privilegiado”

item.description: Momento inolvidable para el astro argentino después de que la Albiceleste estuviera abajo 0-2 en el marcador. Con carácter, esfuerzo y mucho empuje, Argentina logró darle la vuelta al partido y avanzar a los cuartos de final del Mundial 2026.

media:content.media:title: El llanto descontrolado de Lionel Messi tras la épica remontada de Argentina ante Egipto
media:content.media:description: Momento inolvidable para el astro argentino después de que la Albiceleste estuviera abajo 0-2 en el marcador. Con carácter, esfuerzo y mucho empuje, Argentina logró darle la vuelta al partido y avanzar a los cuartos de final del Mundial 2026.

item.title: El llanto descontrolado de Lionel Messi tras la épica remontada de Argentina ante Egipto

item.description: Tras un final cardiaco y una remontada hecha en los últimos 15 minutos del partido ante Egipto, Scaloni no aguanta el llanto de la emoción. El auxiliar Fabián Ayala se conmueve por la resiliencia y pelea que los jugadores argentinos dieron hasta el final.

media:content.media:title: Scaloni: “Qué grupo de jugadores”
media:content.media:description: Tras un final cardiaco y una remontada hecha en los últimos 15 minutos del partido ante Egipto, Scaloni no aguanta el llanto de la emoción. El auxiliar Fabián Ayala se conmueve por la resiliencia y pelea que los jugadores argentinos dieron hasta el final.

item.title: Scaloni: “Qué grupo de jugadores”

item.description: La campeona del mundo, Argentina, remonta en los últimos instantes para llevarse el pase a cuartos de final en Atlanta. Los aficionados están enloquecidos con la heroica actuación de la albiceleste.

media:content.media:title: ¡Argentina está de regreso! La albiceleste remonta en un duelo cardíaco ante Egipto
media:content.media:description: La campeona del mundo, Argentina, remonta en los últimos instantes para llevarse el pase a cuartos de final en Atlanta. Los aficionados están enloquecidos con la heroica actuación de la albiceleste.

item.title: ¡Argentina está de regreso! La albiceleste remonta en un duelo cardíaco ante Egipto

item.description: El mediocampista remató de cabeza en el área tras una contra letal. La selección campeona del mundo dio la vuelta al partido que ganaba 2-0 en los Octavos de Final de la Copa del Mundo.

media:content.media:title: Enzo Fernández anota el 3-2 para dar la vuelta a Egipto
media:content.media:description: El mediocampista remató de cabeza en el área tras una contra letal. La selección campeona del mundo dio la vuelta al partido que ganaba 2-0 en los Octavos de Final de la Copa del Mundo.

item.title: Enzo Fernández anota el 3-2 para dar la vuelta a Egipto

item.description: Apareció el número "10" para poner el empate en un partido donde estaban prácticamente eliminados de la Copa del Mundo 2026.

media:content.media:title: Messi se agranda y marca el 2-2 para Argentina ante Egipto
media:content.media:description: Apareció el número "10" para poner el empate en un partido donde estaban prácticamente eliminados de la Copa del Mundo 2026.

item.title: Messi se agranda y marca el 2-2 para Argentina ante Egipto

item.description: El defensa apareció en el área para sacar un potente remate y descontar para la Albiceleste en un partido de alto voltaje en Atlanta.

media:content.media:title: Cuti Romero anota y da vida a Argentina en contra de Egipto
media:content.media:description: El defensa apareció en el área para sacar un potente remate y descontar para la Albiceleste en un partido de alto voltaje en Atlanta.

item.title: Cuti Romero anota y da vida a Argentina en contra de Egipto

item.description: Con la misma fórmula, Egipto ejecutó una contra perfecta para golpear a la Albiceleste con el 0-2.

media:content.media:title: Mostafa Ziko castiga a Argentina con el 0-2: Los campeones están contra las cuerdas
media:content.media:description: Con la misma fórmula, Egipto ejecutó una contra perfecta para golpear a la Albiceleste con el 0-2.

item.title: Mostafa Ziko castiga a Argentina con el 0-2: Los campeones están contra las cuerdas

item.description: Una contra totalmente letal colocó el segundo tanto del conjunto africano, pero una falta en su propio terreno determinó que el marcador siguiera 0-1.

media:content.media:title: El VAR salva a Argentina y Messi respira
media:content.media:description: Una contra totalmente letal colocó el segundo tanto del conjunto africano, pero una falta en su propio terreno determinó que el marcador siguiera 0-1.

item.title: El VAR salva a Argentina y Messi respira

item.description: A growing number of prominent Democrats are calling on Maine Senate nominee Graham Platner to drop out of the race following a new sexual assault allegation. The claim comes from a former romantic partner who shared details of an incident from five years ago. Platner has explicitly denied the allegations. NBC’s Ryan Nobles reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: Calls Grow for Graham Platner to Quit Race Over New Allegation
media:content.media:description: A growing number of prominent Democrats are calling on Maine Senate nominee Graham Platner to drop out of the race following a new sexual assault allegation. The claim comes from a former romantic partner who shared details of an incident from five years ago. Platner has explicitly denied the allegations. NBC’s Ryan Nobles reports for TODAY.

item.title: Calls Grow for Graham Platner to Quit Race Over New Allegation

item.description: President Donald Trump met with Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan to kick off the NATO summit. NBC News’ Monica Alba reports that Trump addressed the sale of F-35 jets to Turkey and reaching a conclusion in the Russia-Ukraine war.

media:content.media:title: Trump talks Ukraine-Russia and F-35 jets sale in meeting with Turkey’s Erdoğan
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump met with Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan to kick off the NATO summit. NBC News’ Monica Alba reports that Trump addressed the sale of F-35 jets to Turkey and reaching a conclusion in the Russia-Ukraine war.

item.title: Trump talks Ukraine-Russia and F-35 jets sale in meeting with Turkey’s Erdoğan

item.description: New York City officials said that a 37-story Manhattan building remains unstable after two structural support beams were reported to be buckling, prompting an emergency response. Mayor Zohran Mamdani said stabilization work would begin as soon as the floor is deemed safe.

media:content.media:title: 'The building remains unstable': Mamdani briefs on high-rise with buckling beams
media:content.media:description: New York City officials said that a 37-story Manhattan building remains unstable after two structural support beams were reported to be buckling, prompting an emergency response. Mayor Zohran Mamdani said stabilization work would begin as soon as the floor is deemed safe.

item.title: 'The building remains unstable': Mamdani briefs on high-rise with buckling beams

item.dc:creator: Frank Thorp V
Scott Wong
item.description: As rumors have swirled about Sen. Mitch McConnell’s health, Senate Republican leaders and a former adviser say they talked to him on the phone from the hospital.

media:content.media:description: Mitch McConnell, R-Ky., arrives to chair the Senate Appropriations Subcommittee on Defense hearing on the budget request for the U.S. Army on May 19, 2026.

item.title: Senate leaders say they’ve spoken to Mitch McConnell in the hospital

item.dc:creator: Steve Kopack
item.description: Oil prices jumped more than 3% after reports that two tankers transiting the Strait of Hormuz had been hit by unknown projectiles.

media:content.media:text: Vessels at the Strait of Hormuz, as seen from Musandam
media:content.media:description: Three vessels in or near the Strait of Hormuz were reportedly hit by projectiles that a U.S. official said were fired by Iran, all in under 24 hours.

item.title: Oil prices rise as attacks revive safety concerns in the Strait of Hormuz

item.dc:creator: Chantal Da Silva
item.description: Far-right leader Marine Le Pen will be able to run in France’s presidential election next year, though her involvement remained in doubt Tuesday after a Paris appeals court upheld her conviction for misusing European Union funds.

media:content.media:description: Marine Le Pen on Wednesday.

item.title: French court allows far-right leader Marine Le Pen to run for president with ankle tag

item.description: The New Year's Eve ball in Times Square has dropped for America's 250th anniversary, making this the first time the ball has dropped outside of a New Year's celebration.

media:content.media:title: New Year's Eve ball drops in Times Square for America's 250th
media:content.media:description: The New Year's Eve ball in Times Square has dropped for America's 250th anniversary, making this the first time the ball has dropped outside of a New Year's celebration.

item.title: New Year's Eve ball drops in Times Square for America's 250th

item.dc:creator: Frank Thorp V
Scott Wong
item.description: As rumors have swirled about Sen. Mitch McConnell’s health, Senate Republican leaders and a former adviser say they talked to him on the phone from the hospital.

media:content.media:description: Mitch McConnell, R-Ky., arrives to chair the Senate Appropriations Subcommittee on Defense hearing on the budget request for the U.S. Army on May 19, 2026.

item.title: Senate leaders say they’ve spoken to Mitch McConnell in the hospital

item.dc:creator: Gary Grumbach
Dan De Luce
Nicole Acevedo
item.description: The Iranian American Legal Defense Fund stated that the alleged sharing of information violates asylum seekers’ confidentiality and endangers them and their family members.

media:content.media:text: People walk the streets of Tehran.
media:content.media:description: People walk the streets of Tehran, Iran, on June 18.

item.title: U.S. shared confidential immigration details with Iranian government, lawsuit alleges

item.dc:creator: David K. Li
item.description: Items discarded behind a Cracker Barrel restaurant in Georgia played a key role in helping Ohio prosecutors secure an arrest in connection with the killing of a traveling salesman more than 40 years ago, authorities said Monday.

media:content.media:text: John Warren was found dead in his hotel room in Middletown, Ohio, in 1985.
media:content.media:description: John Warren was found dead in his hotel room in Middletown, Ohio, in 1985.

item.title: Items left behind Cracker Barrel lead to arrest in 1985 death of a salesman

item.dc:creator: Steve Kopack
item.description: Oil prices jumped more than 3% after reports that two tankers transiting the Strait of Hormuz had been hit by unknown projectiles.

media:content.media:text: Vessels at the Strait of Hormuz, as seen from Musandam
media:content.media:description: Three vessels in or near the Strait of Hormuz were reportedly hit by projectiles that a U.S. official said were fired by Iran, all in under 24 hours.

item.title: Oil prices rise as attacks revive safety concerns in the Strait of Hormuz

item.dc:creator: Matt Lavietes
Emilie Ikeda
item.description: A high-rise apartment complex in Midtown Manhattan remains structurally unstable and is at risk of collapse after the building’s support columns started buckling on Tuesday morning, officials said.

media:content.media:description: NYPD officers talk about buildings in the area that were evacuated, on July 7, 2026, in New York.

media:content.media:text: A building has been evacuated in New York City
media:content.media:description: A building has been evacuated in New York City on Tuesday.

item.title: Mass evacuations ordered as buckling Manhattan high-rise remains unstable, mayor says

item.dc:creator: Samantha Elkins
item.description: AI company Anthropic announced that it is moving its popular Claude Cowork agent to the cloud, making it accessible to users from multiple devices.

media:content.media:description: Attendees at Anthropic's Code with Claude developer conference in San Francisco on May 6.

item.title: Anthropic will make Claude Cowork available to users via the cloud

item.dc:creator: David Hickey
item.description: The United States’ World Cup campaign was brought to a screeching halt in Seattle last night by a Belgian team that played as if it had extra motivation after the Folarin Balogun affair.

media:content.media:text: USA v Belgium: Round of 16 - FIFA World Cup 2026
media:content.media:description: Romelu Lukaku #9 of Belgium celebrates with teammates after scoring the team's fourth goal during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 16 match between USA and Belgium at Seattle Stadium on July 06, 2026 in Seattle, Washington.

media:content.media:text: Mauricio Pochettino, Head Coach of the United States, speaks with players in a huddle after the 1-4 loss during the FIFA World Cup 2026.
media:content.media:description: Mauricio Pochettino, head coach of the United States, speaks with players in a huddle after the 1-4 loss against Belgium at Seattle Stadium on July 6.

media:content.media:text: Lionel Messi runs in celebration near a soccer net on the field
media:content.media:description: Lionel Messi of Argentina celebrates after scoring his team's second goal against Austria on June 22.

item.title: From The Sports Desk: Belgium demolishes the U.S. 4-1, and then trolls it afterward
